name: Genghis Khan
fact1: Born into a nomadic clan, he was reportedly named after a rival his father had captured
fact2: He united warring tribes and ruled through a feared cavalry and a continent-spanning courier network
fact3: His conquests formed the largest contiguous land empire ever, from the Pacific to eastern Europe

name: Cleopatra
fact1: She belonged to a Greek-speaking dynasty that had ruled for nearly three centuries
fact2: Famously multilingual, she was said to be the first of her line to learn her subjects' own language
fact3: She allied with and romanced two of Rome's most powerful men before her kingdom fell to Rome

name: Albert Einstein
fact1: He was a restless, self-driven thinker who chafed against rote schooling
fact2: He developed era-defining theories while working as a clerk in a patent office
fact3: His theory of relativity and the link between energy and mass remade modern physics

name: Marie Curie
fact1: She left her homeland to get an education that was closed to women there
fact2: She shared a landmark science prize with her husband and research partner
fact3: She discovered new radioactive elements and won Nobel Prizes in two different sciences

name: Napoleon Bonaparte
fact1: Born on a Mediterranean island, he rose fast through the army amid revolution
fact2: A gifted general, he seized power in a coup and later crowned himself ruler
fact3: He dominated Europe until a ruinous invasion of Russia and final defeat at Waterloo

name: Leonardo da Vinci
fact1: A self-taught polymath, he left behind more unfinished projects than completed ones
fact2: His notebooks, written in mirror script, sketched flying machines and human anatomy
fact3: He painted the world's most famous portrait and a celebrated Last Supper

name: Nelson Mandela
fact1: Trained as a lawyer, he became the face of a resistance movement
fact2: He spent 27 years in prison for fighting a system of racial segregation
fact3: After his release he won the Nobel Peace Prize and became his nation's first Black president

name: Confucius
fact1: A teacher and minor official, his ideas were gathered by students after his death
fact2: He taught that social harmony flows from respect and the duties binding ruler and subject
fact3: His sayings, collected as the Analects, founded a philosophy that shaped East Asia for ages
